title: Anthropic identifies industrial-scale distillation attacks by DeepSeek, Moonshot AI, and MiniMax
why_it_matters: Anthropic publicly names three Chinese labs for creating 24,000+ fraudulent accounts and 16M+ exchanges to distill its models — major industry security incident with geopolitical implications.

title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.4 with Pro and Thinking versions
why_it_matters: Major frontier model release from OpenAI — GPT-5.4 combines reasoning, coding, and agentic capabilities into one model, with Pro and Thinking variants available in API and ChatGPT.

title: GPT-5.4 Thinking and GPT-5.4 Pro rolling out in ChatGPT and API
why_it_matters: Official OpenAI announcement confirms GPT-5.4 is live across ChatGPT, API, and Codex — cross-source confirmation of the biggest model launch this week.

title: Introducing Claude Sonnet 4.6
why_it_matters: Anthropic releases Sonnet 4.6 with frontier performance across coding, agents, and professional work — directly competing with GPT-5.4 in the same news cycle.

title: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite: Built for intelligence at scale
why_it_matters: Google's most cost-efficient Gemini 3 model targets high-volume production use cases — signals the race moving from raw capability to cost-efficiency at scale.

title: Quantifying infrastructure noise in agentic coding evals
why_it_matters: Anthropic shows infrastructure configuration can swing agentic coding benchmarks by several percentage points — sometimes more than the gap between top models on leaderboards. Essential reading for eval methodology.

title: Pentagon Formally Labels Anthropic Supply-Chain Risk
why_it_matters: The Pentagon escalates its conflict with Anthropic by formally labeling it a supply-chain risk — unprecedented move that could reshape how AI companies engage with government contracts.

title: Latest open artifacts (#19): Qwen 3.5, GLM 5, MiniMax 2.5 — Chinese labs' latest push of the frontier
why_it_matters: Comprehensive analysis of three major Chinese lab releases hitting open-source simultaneously — Qwen 3.5, GLM 5, and MiniMax 2.5 represent a coordinated frontier push from China.

title: Cursor rolls out Automations: a new agentic coding tool
why_it_matters: Cursor's Automations let users auto-launch coding agents triggered by codebase changes, Slack messages, or timers — moves agentic coding from interactive to ambient/autonomous.

title: New in Claude Code: Remote Control — kick off tasks in terminal, continue from phone
why_it_matters: Claude Code's Remote Control lets developers start coding tasks in terminal and monitor/control from mobile — signals shift toward truly asynchronous AI-assisted development.

title: Qwen/Qwen3.5-35B-A3B (MoE, image-text-to-text)
why_it_matters: Qwen 3.5's 35B MoE model with only 3B active params is trending hard on HuggingFace — efficient multimodal architecture that could run on consumer hardware. Also trending: Qwen3-Coder-Next (1065 likes, 1M+ downloads) and GLM-5 (1713 likes).

title: A GitHub Issue Title Compromised 4k Developer Machines
why_it_matters: Prompt injection via a GitHub issue title compromised 4,000 developer machines through AI coding tools — critical security lesson for anyone using agentic coding assistants.

title: Luma launches creative AI agents powered by 'Unified Intelligence' models
why_it_matters: Luma Agents coordinate multiple AI systems for end-to-end creative work across text, images, video, and audio — agentic AI expanding from code into creative production.

title: Nano Banana 2: Combining Pro capabilities with lightning-fast speed
why_it_matters: Google's latest image generation model offers advanced world knowledge and subject consistency at Flash speed — production-ready image gen is now fast enough for real-time applications.

title: LiquidAI/LFM2-24B-A2B: Liquid Foundation Model 2
why_it_matters: LiquidAI's non-transformer architecture model trending on HuggingFace — alternative architectures gaining traction as the field explores beyond standard transformers.

title: NotebookLM can now summarize research in 'cinematic' video overviews
why_it_matters: Google's NotebookLM evolves from audio podcasts to fully animated cinematic videos — raises the bar for AI-generated content from structured sources.

title: Can coding agents relicense open source through 'clean room' implementation?
why_it_matters: Simon Willison examines whether AI coding agents can circumvent open-source licenses via 'clean room' reimplementation — a legal and ethical question that will define AI-assisted development norms.

title: Olmo Hybrid and future LLM architectures
why_it_matters: Deep analysis of hybrid architectures in the latest Olmo model and open-source post-training tools — signals where frontier open models are heading architecturally.

title: Netflix is buying Ben Affleck's AI startup InterPositive
why_it_matters: Netflix acquires an AI production startup — signals major entertainment companies moving from AI experimentation to acquisition-driven integration into content pipelines.

title: LangSmith CLI & Skills
why_it_matters: LangChain releases CLI tools and 'skills' that give AI coding agents expertise in their ecosystem — part of the growing trend of frameworks creating agent-native interfaces.

title: Online harassment is entering its AI era
why_it_matters: AI agents are now being used to overwhelm open-source maintainers with spam contributions — a new attack vector where AI harassment scales beyond what human moderation can handle.
